critical.title: Mobile header: cramped/unstable action cluster risk (wrapping/clipping) due to tight critical CSS constraints
critical.evidence: Code: inline critical CSS in `wp-content/themes/mnsk7-storefront/header.php` sets `.mnsk7-header__inner{gap:1rem;padding:0 1rem;min-height:52px}` and logo image `max-height:42px`. Hypothesis: at 390px width this can force wrap/clipping when cart count visible.
critical.repro_steps: Mobile 390x700: open home + PDP + cart + checkout. Check header stays 1-row; no overlaps; cart count badge does not clip; no horizontal scroll.
critical.expected: Single-row header, stable spacing, all primary actions tappable.
critical.suggested_fix: Theme: make header layout explicitly nowrap on mobile; reduce gaps at <=390; cap logo width; ensure action cluster has fixed hit areas and doesn't wrap.

critical.title: PDP action cluster: stock/qty/add-to-cart can wrap into unclear purchase block on mobile
critical.evidence: Code: PDP buybox is customized (`woocommerce/content-single-product.php` + `assets/css/parts/06-single-product.css`); variation add-to-cart is flex-wrap; touch targets enforce 44px min height (`assets/css/parts/22-touch-targets.css`). Hypothesis: combined min-heights + wrap => misalignment/weak CTA clarity.
critical.repro_steps: Mobile 390x700: open variable PDP, select variation, verify qty + button grouping is coherent and button is fully visible/enabled.
critical.expected: One cohesive purchase cluster; predictable stacking; no cramped wrap.
critical.suggested_fix: Theme CSS: define explicit mobile layout for add-to-cart cluster (stack button full-width, qty next to it or above; consistent gaps; align availability inline).

critical.title: Sticky PDP CTA: enforce bottom safe-area so last interactive element is never obscured (recos/footer)
critical.evidence: Code: sticky CTA exists in `woocommerce/content-single-product.php` as `#mnsk7-pdp-sticky-cta` (hidden by default). Hypothesis: when shown it can cover recos/footer unless bottom spacing is reserved.
critical.repro_steps: Mobile 390x700: on a PDP where sticky CTA becomes visible, scroll to bottom; verify last related card CTA/footer link is fully visible and tappable (not under sticky).
critical.expected: No occlusion; bottom padding compensates sticky height + safe-area inset.
critical.suggested_fix: Theme CSS: reserve bottom padding using a CSS var for sticky height; optionally hide/compact sticky near footer/related section end.

medium.title: PLP first screen: products + filter/sort entry are delayed by header/chips/controls (dead space)
medium.evidence: Code pointers: PLP layout split across `assets/css/parts/24-plp-table.css`, `05-plp-cards.css`, `25-global-layout.css` and archive template `woocommerce/archive-product.php`. Hypothesis: stacked controls/chips reduce above-fold product density.
medium.repro_steps: Mobile 390x700: open /sklep/ and a category/tag. Check how quickly first product card appears and whether filter/sort entry is obvious.
medium.expected: Catalog-first: first product appears early; controls compact.
medium.suggested_fix: Theme: compress PLP header/chips on mobile; convert chips to one scroll row or collapsed drawer; keep sort/filter in compact bar.

medium.title: PDP related/upsells cards: ragged title wrapping and uneven hierarchy reduce scanability (card composition)
medium.evidence: Code pointers: card styling in `assets/css/parts/12-related-products.css` and shared PLP card styles (`05-plp-cards.css`). Hypothesis: titles with separators wrap unpredictably; CTA alignment inconsistent.
medium.repro_steps: Mobile 390x700: on PDP scroll to related/upsells. Check title wrapping consistency, card heights, price/CTA placement alignment.
medium.expected: Predictable card rhythm: 2-line clamp title, consistent price + CTA placement, uniform heights.
medium.suggested_fix: Theme CSS: line-clamp titles; normalize spacing; set consistent thumbnail ratio; align CTA to bottom.

medium.title: Cart first screen: checkout CTA should be visible without scroll (mobile)
medium.evidence: Code pointers: cart/checkout styles in `assets/css/parts/18-cart-checkout.css` + Woo templates.
medium.repro_steps: Mobile 390x700: open /koszyk/ with item. Verify checkout button visible above fold.
medium.suggested_fix: Theme CSS: reorder/compact cart blocks on mobile; optionally sticky summary.

medium.title: Checkout mobile: enforce single-column and compact notice stack to prioritize form start
medium.evidence: Code pointers: checkout styles in `assets/css/parts/18-cart-checkout.css` + notices `16-woo-notices.css`.
medium.repro_steps: Mobile 390x700: open /zamowienie/. Verify form fields start quickly and layout is single column.
medium.suggested_fix: Theme CSS: force single column; reduce notice padding/typography on mobile.

medium.title: Taste: header should feel task-first (technical-industrial commerce), not desktop scaled-down
medium.evidence: Heuristic/taste hypothesis based on header structure and dense action cluster on mobile.
medium.repro_steps: Mobile 390x700: evaluate header focus vs products (does header dominate?)
medium.suggested_fix: Theme: mobile header variant (collapse nav links into one trigger; keep search/cart).

minor.title: Home first screen: hero + primary entry to shop/catalog should read clearly without excess header dead space
minor.evidence: Orchestrator targets home hero/first screen; gap vs PLP/PDP focus. Hypothesis: header + hero stack may push first actionable content down.
minor.repro_steps: Mobile 390x700: open home. Note first screen: header height, hero, first CTA/link to shop.
minor.expected: Task-first: user sees brand + clear path to catalog within first screen.
minor.suggested_fix: Theme: tighten hero top margin on mobile if needed; ensure one obvious primary CTA.

minor.title: Footer: link groups and tap spacing readable on mobile (no cramped columns)
minor.evidence: Sticky CTA issue references footer visibility; footer is end-of-flow anchor for trust/links.
minor.repro_steps: Mobile 390x700: scroll to footer on home + PDP. Check link tap areas and wrapping.
minor.expected: Footer links tappable; adequate spacing; no horizontal scroll.
minor.suggested_fix: Theme CSS: single-column or stacked footer groups on narrow widths.

medium.title: LCP risk (hypothesis): home/PLP first paint dominated by header inline critical CSS + hero imagery
medium.evidence: Header uses inline critical CSS; LCP element may be logo or hero image depending on template.
verify_plan: Lighthouse navigation (mobile): record LCP element and time; repeat on / and /sklep/
If LCP is hero image: confirm width/height or aspect-ratio to avoid late resize

medium.title: CLS / jank risk (hypothesis): sticky PDP CTA show/hide or cart badge updates shift layout
medium.evidence: Sticky CTA toggles visibility; Woo notices and header cart count can inject height changes.
verify_plan: Performance panel: CLS during add-to-cart and scroll on PDP
Scroll to related/footer: check layout thrash when sticky appears

medium.title: Mobile header: icon-only controls (search, menu, cart) need visible labels or aria-label consistent with purpose
medium.evidence: Dense action cluster; WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value — icons without text must expose accessible names.
verify_plan: axe DevTools or equivalent: critical/serious violations on home + PDP
Screen reader spot-check: cart and search announce shop-relevant names
medium.repro_steps: Inspect header controls in accessibility tree; tab through focus order.
medium.expected: Each control has descriptive accessible name; focus visible.
medium.suggested_fix: Theme: aria-label on icon links/buttons; ensure one h1 per page elsewhere.

medium.title: When sticky CTA is visible, last focusable content (related/footer links) must remain reachable and not covered
medium.evidence: Keyboard/switch users can reach footer; overlap is both UI and a11y failure (2.4.7 Focus visible / obstruction).
verify_plan: Keyboard: Tab from main content through related and footer with sticky CTA shown
Check scroll-padding-bottom / focus scroll-into-view not hidden under sticky
medium.repro_steps: Keyboard-only: on PDP scroll until sticky appears; continue to footer links.
medium.expected: No focused element hidden under sticky bar; scroll-padding or layout reserves space.
medium.suggested_fix: Theme CSS: scroll-padding-bottom; match ui-sticky-cta-collision-recos fix.

add_to_cart.notes: PDP action cluster layout + sticky CTA behavior are primary risks on mobile.

cart_update.notes: Cart CTA visibility and control spacing on mobile needs validation.

checkout_entry.notes: Header and cart/checkout above-fold composition can block or delay checkout entry.

cta.pass_criteria: Primary CTAs (add to cart, proceed to checkout, place order entry) are visible, not clipped, min ~44px hit area; sticky/header do not permanently cover them.
cta.fail_criteria: Purchase or checkout CTA off-screen without alternative; CTA overlapped by sticky bar or header overflow.

add_to_cart.pass_criteria: Simple/variable PDP: quantity + add-to-cart form one coherent cluster; button enabled when purchasable; no broken wrap hiding controls.
add_to_cart.fail_criteria: Add to cart missing, disabled without cause, or layout wrap hides qty/button; variation selection unusable.

cart.pass_criteria: Header cart icon/count readable; /koszyk/ shows line items and checkout CTA in first screen on typical cart.
cart.fail_criteria: Cart badge clipped; cannot open cart; checkout CTA requires scroll on normal 1–3 line cart.

checkout_entry.pass_criteria: From cart, path to /zamowienie/ is clear; checkout form and notices stack in single column; form fields start without excessive scroll.
checkout_entry.fail_criteria: Wrong column order; notices push form below fold; layout blocks completing customer details.

top_priorities.why: Woo woo_blocking_map.cta + a11y: sticky must not occlude recos/footer or last CTAs.
Woo woo_blocking_map.add_to_cart: purchase cluster must stay coherent on mobile.
Woo woo_blocking_map.cart: global header/cart stability on all routes.
Icon-only header controls need names before tightening layout further.
Woo woo_blocking_map.checkout_entry: form must win first screen.
